findings.summary: BudgetBuilderPage lacks an integration test unlike the other 3 page-level integration tests
evidence: GroupsPage, BudgetPage, and SyncPage each have .integration.test.jsx files that render real child components
BudgetBuilderPage.test.jsx mocks all 3 child components (BuilderProfileForm, BuilderRegionalData, BuilderResultsTable) but has no integration test counterpart
This means the real prop-passing and data flow from BudgetBuilderPage to its children is never verified in tests
findings.suggestion: Add BudgetBuilderPage.integration.test.jsx that renders with real child components (mocking only recharts/useResponsive) to verify parent-to-child data flow, matching the pattern of the other 3 integration tests

findings.summary: mockFetch substring matching can silently match wrong routes when URL prefixes overlap
evidence: mockFetch uses url.includes(key) — '/api/budget-builder/regional' matches both the GET endpoint and '/api/budget-builder/regional/fetch'
Tests that need both routes (e.g. BudgetBuilderPage handleFetchRegionalAI) work around this by replacing global.fetch entirely with vi.fn() instead of using mockFetch
Route match order depends on Object.entries iteration order, creating implicit ordering dependencies
findings.suggestion: Sort routes by descending key length in mockFetch so longer/more-specific routes match first, or switch to exact path matching with optional query string handling

findings.summary: NetWorthChart range button test only checks no crash, not that range filtering actually occurred
evidence: Line 48-53: test 'clicking a range button does not crash' only asserts the chart container still exists after click
No assertion that the active range state changed, the correct range is highlighted, or that data was filtered
findings.suggestion: Assert the clicked range button receives an active class or aria-pressed attribute, or verify the active range label in the RangeSelector child
